label_source: Layer-1 resolve pattern cmp,vp,vpc,naive = _,W,W,L : value_profile AND value_profile_cmplog RESOLVE, naive BLOCKS, cmplog non-decisive (_). The only technique delta between the involved winner (vp/vpc) and loser (naive) is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback (naive has edge-only feedback). The shape's Layer-1 attribution is therefore VP CMP_MAP gradient; this test is the INDEPENDENT real-data verification (G2) of HOW that gradient advantage manifests in the value_profile-RESOLVING vs naive-BLOCKING corpus content.

round_history.round0: Two MEASUREMENT MODES of one gradient claim: vp_cmpmap_gradient_distance_closure (value_distance_reached) validated 5 fixed-operand branches (lcms x4 + sqlite3/14408); vp_cmpmap_gradient_depth_reach was an UNBUILT seed_set parse-proxy that never scored 4 branches. Distance-by-proxy DEAD for the inconclusive 9.
round_history.round2: Pivoted to TOKEN DENSITY (vp_admits_token_dense_corpus, struct token-per-byte) which confirmed 3 SQL grammar branches, plus vp_admits_larger_valid_structure (seed bigness, font only) which confirmed 2 (harfbuzz). After round-2 arbitration 10 branches are CONFIRMED+FROZEN; 4 remained rule_not_met (mechanism not supported by the round-2 measurements).
round_history.round3_target: The 4 still-unlabeled rule_not_met branches share one trait the prior 3 measurements are structurally BLIND to: they are RANGE / LOOP / DEEP-DISPATCH gates with NO harvestable single fixed-byte operand at a fixed offset (so scalar Hamming distance H1 is N/A or chance-prone at width<=2) and they are NOT token-density-separated (H2 refuted) nor font-bigness (H3, font only). Their mechanism is explicitly ITERATION / PARSE DEPTH: longer in-range digit runs that push a loop counter past a threshold, a hex-charref loop reaching its non-hex terminator, the shaper driving the codepoint switch to actually take a deep case arm. The round-3 pivot is the BUILT depth_reach tool (run seeds through libafl-<target>-cov, read the branch-line per-seed execution depth) -- a genuinely different real-data signature than distance / density / size.

round0_outcome.validated_via: vp_cmpmap_distance_closure_fixed_operand (value_distance_reached: naive_min_distance>0 AND distance_closure_ratio>=0.5) -- FIXED-operand value gates (lcms FOURCCs, sqlite3 '||'); CARRIED FORWARD unchanged so the round-3 pivot does NOT regress them.

hypotheses.label: For gates with a SINGLE fixed comparison operand, value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient admits a corpus that lands at a strictly smaller residual Hamming/prefix distance to that operand than the naive-blocking corpus ever reaches. (CARRIED FORWARD; already validated for 5 branches -- kept to avoid regression.)

params.note: Operand from signature.operand_literal + operand_width_bytes. Run by whichever server owns the corpus (lcms on sB; sqlite3 on s4). All 5 HELD this in round-0; carried forward verbatim.

hypotheses.prediction: naive_min_distance > 0 (the naive-blocking corpus systematically STALLS at a positive residual to the fixed operand -- carries 'RGB ' not the FOURCC, no '||') while value_profile closes it, so distance_closure_ratio is large. DISCRIMINATING vs a no-gradient null (VP got the operand by the same blind luck as naive), which predicts naive_min_distance ~ vp_min_distance.

hypotheses.label: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient admits a token-DENSE corpus: the VP-resolving seed set carries far more of the gate's required grammar/structural tokens PER BYTE than the naive-blocking set, because the gradient rewards concentrated partial-assembly and prunes bloat, whereas naive's edge-only feedback accumulates large, token-SPARSE byte-garbage. (CARRIED FORWARD; validated round-2 for the SQL-grammar subset.)

params.comment: Reuses the BUILT joint_necessity struct compute (seed_bytes loader + struct_stats + analyze_pair winner=value_profile/loser=naive) but EMITS one new metric: token_density = mean over a fuzzer's seeds of (count of --tokens present) / (seed length in bytes). token_density_lift = vp_token_density / naive_token_density. registry_tool=null -> orchestrator parameterizes the existing seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count:tokens:per_branch descriptor to emit density (not a new file).

tokens_by_branch.sqlite3/14588: SELECT,FROM,*,<<,%,(,),- -- deep arithmetic-expression operators over numeric literals.

params.scope: ON-DISK CORPUS REQUIRED (s4 holds sqlite3). Needs >=3 value_profile resolving + >=3 naive blocking seeds; sqlite3/14745_h0 with VP W=2 may fall to seed_starved (H5).

hypotheses.prediction: token_density_lift >= 2.0 (VP corpus carries at least 2x more grammar tokens per byte than naive). DISCRIMINATING vs the refuted scalar-distance story (density counts whole-token PRESENCE per byte, orthogonal to residual Hamming distance), vs the falsified bigness story (here VP seeds are SMALLER), and vs a no-gradient null (token_density_lift ~ 1.0).

hypotheses.label: For STRUCTURAL FORMAT-INTEGRITY gates (sfnt/font assembly), value_profile's gradient admits a corpus of PHYSICALLY LARGER, valid-header seeds than the naive-blocking corpus -- the gradient toward well-formed table directories produces bigger, more-tokened inputs. (CARRIED FORWARD; validated round-2 for harfbuzz/5302.)

measurement.tool_mode: analyze_pair(winner_fz=value_profile, loser_fz=naive) -- __WWL has value_profile RESOLVING and naive BLOCKING; the existing analyze_pair emits winner_/loser_/tag_lift/size_lift, no code change.

params.scope: harfbuzz on-disk on s4; W=value_profile(10) L=naive(10). Round-2 confirmed size_lift=4.69, tag_lift=1.0.

hypotheses.prediction: size_lift >= 1.3 AND tag_lift >= 1.0 : the VP-resolving font corpus is >=30% larger AND carries >=1 more valid sfnt table tag than the naive-blocking corpus. DISCRIMINATING: a no-gradient null predicts size_lift ~ 1.0 AND tag_lift ~ 0; the SQL/grammar branches FALSIFY the bigness direction (VP smaller), so this rule is the FONT-assembly signature, not a generic 'VP bigger'.

hypotheses.label: NEW (round-3 pivot). For RANGE / LOOP / DEEP-DISPATCH gates with NO harvestable single fixed-byte operand (digit-range loops past a counter threshold, a hex-charref loop reaching its non-hex terminator, a deep codepoint switch arm), value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient admits a corpus that drives the gate's branch LINE to a materially greater per-seed EXECUTION DEPTH than the naive-blocking corpus, which stalls shallow. The gradient is visible NOT as residual operand distance (no fixed operand) nor token-per-byte density nor seed size, but as how DEEP / how many TIMES the average winner seed drives the branch line through the instrumented binary.
replaces: vp_cmpmap_gradient_depth_reach (round-0 UNBUILT seed_set parse-proxy -- now realized by the BUILT depth_reach Docker/llvm-cov tool)

measurement.tool_mode: branch(--winner-fuzzer value_profile --loser-fuzzer naive) -- runs the value_profile-RESOLVING vs naive-BLOCKING seeds through libafl-<target>-cov + per_role_in_container.py, reads the branch line's per-seed-normalized llvm-cov execution count. Needs >=1 W and >=1 L on-disk seed (NOT >=3 -- depth normalizes per seed), so it scores the seed-starved harfbuzz/6616 (VP W=2) that distance/density/size could not.

params.rule_metric_note: Rule keys on depth_lift (per-seed line-execution depth ratio) + winner_deeper, NOT on side_lift. Smoke on harfbuzz/6616 showed side_lift=0.91 (the True/False arm count at a SWITCH dispatch line is noisy and NON-discriminating), while depth_lift=3.0 / winner_deeper=true cleanly separates -- so the decision rule must use line-execution depth, not the blocked-side count, for switch/loop gates.

depth_proxy_by_branch.libxml2/6429: branch line in xmlParseCharRef hex loop (the else->xmlFatalErr arm / case XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF): VP corpus drives the hex-digit loop deep enough to present a non-hex terminator; naive's '<?xml ..?>' docs never enter the loop. sB-owned corpus.
depth_proxy_by_branch.libxml2/6461: line 2317 NXT(2)=='x' hex-charref opener: VP corpus reaches the HEX path (lines 2318-2344) -> branch line executed/taken; naive stalls on the decimal '&#' else-if (line 2349) and the line's true side is never taken. sB-owned corpus.
depth_proxy_by_branch.libxml2/6467_h1: line 2353 'if (count++ > 20)' inside the decimal digit-run loop: VP corpus grows long in-range digit runs (analysis: W branch hitcount 8 vs L 1) so the loop LINE is executed many more times per seed and the reset branch trips; naive enters the loop only ~4 times and exits shallow. sB-owned corpus.
depth_proxy_by_branch.harfbuzz/6616: line 309 'case 0x0D91u' codepoint switch in the vowel-constraints shaper: VP corpus drives the shaper to reach line 309 (smoke: winner_depth=1.5 vs loser_depth=0.17, depth_lift=3.0, winner_deeper=true) -- the value_profile seeds carry the Sinhala-script bytes that reach the deep case dispatch, naive's garbage shapes to a buffer that never reaches it. s4-owned corpus; scored on s4 despite VP W=2 (depth_reach needs only >=1 W/L).

params.scope: Docker per-branch (~30-60s) against libafl-<target>-cov. harfbuzz/6616 scores on s4 (cov image present, W=2/L=6 verified). The three libxml2 branches are sB-owned (libafl-libxml2-cov + libxml2 seeds live on sB; Dockerfile.libxml2.cov exists) -- they are designed-valid and ARBITRATED BY sB, NOT marked decidable:false merely because absent on s4 (re-design rule #4).

hypotheses.prediction: winner_depth > loser_depth with depth_lift >= 1.5 : the value_profile-resolving corpus drives the gate's branch line to a materially greater per-seed execution depth than the naive-blocking corpus, which stalls shallow (naive's digit run never reaches the loop-counter threshold; naive's input never reaches the hex path / the deep case arm). DISCRIMINATING on TWO fronts: (1) vs the prior-3 measurements which are STRUCTURALLY BLIND here -- there is no single fixed operand (distance H1 N/A / chance-prone at width<=2), no token-density separation (H2 refuted for these), and no font-size signature (H3 font-only); depth captures the CUMULATIVE iteration/parse gradient those scalar/byte measurements cannot. (2) vs a no-gradient null (VP got lucky on the final byte, the shape label is noise), which predicts depth_lift ~ 1.0 (both corpora reach the line equally shallow); only a real CMP_MAP gradient that admits progressively deeper in-range/in-grammar seeds predicts VP's corpus is systematically DEEPER per seed. Smoke harfbuzz/6616 -> depth_lift=3.0, winner_deeper=true -> expected to HOLD.

hypotheses.label: honest non-discriminable residue: branches whose value_profile-RESOLVING arm has too few on-disk seeds for the applicable seed/struct tool to compute a stable median (token-density H2 needs >=3 W), AND that have no fixed operand (H1 N/A) nor font-size signature (H3) -- so no BUILT measurement can independently confirm VP's mechanism without over-claiming. (depth_reach H4 only needs >=1 W, so a branch is in this residue ONLY if even its depth signal is absent/unscorable.)

params.note: sqlite3/14658 (VP W=2) and sqlite3/14745_h0 (VP W=2) are seed-starved for the value_profile arm under H2's >=3 requirement. They are SQL deep-parse / wildcard gates: candidate for H4 depth_reach (needs only >=1 W) on a future pass IF a rank-10 re-bisect or the depth signal separates; until then they are honestly left inconclusive. They are NOT marked decidable:false for corpus-absence (their corpus is on-disk on s4) -- they are seed-STARVED for the chosen measurement, the correct G3 outcome. harfbuzz/6616 MOVED OUT of this residue into H4 (depth_reach scores it at W=2).

hypotheses.prediction: N/A -- the applicable seed/struct measurement lacks the >=3 winner seeds to compute a stable statistic. An honest inconclusive beats a rule the data cannot support.
hypotheses.rule: decidable:false -> arbiter leaves these inconclusive (fallback) unless a seed re-bisect raises the VP arm to >=3 (re-enters H2) or H4 depth_reach is run for them and separates (depth_lift>=1.5 AND winner_deeper).

data_realities_respected: value_profile (CMP_MAP) is a FEEDBACK not a mutator -> NEVER measured via a seed_lineage 'gradient event'. All four decidable hypotheses read what the value_profile-RESOLVING vs naive-BLOCKING corpus CONTAINS or REACHES: residual operand distance (H1), token density (H2), structural size+tags (H3), and per-seed branch-line execution DEPTH (H4, depth_reach runs the corpus through the cov binary and reads how deep the AVERAGE seed drives the gate line -- corpus-reach, not a lineage event) (data_realities #1,#2).
I2S is NOT tagged in mutation_op -> NO seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence proposed.

joint/structural-assembly gates have DIFFERENT-SIZED winning vs blocking seeds -> byte_freq_ratio (operand_enrichment) is NOT used: H4 (depth_reach) sidesteps byte-alignment entirely by reading the branch-line execution depth from llvm-cov, which is size-invariant; round-2 confirmed enrichment returns no_gate_signature for these range/loop gates (data_realities #3).

on-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl,harfbuzz,openthread,sqlite3 ON s4; sB holds lcms,libxml2,libpng,bloaty. H1 runs on the corpus owner (lcms on sB, sqlite3 on s4). H2/H3 read on-disk seeds (sqlite3+harfbuzz on s4). H4 depth_reach needs libafl-<target>-cov + on-disk seeds: harfbuzz/6616 on s4 (image present, W=2/L=6 verified, smoke depth_lift=3.0); the three libxml2 branches (6429,6461,6467_h1) are sB-owned -> designed-valid and ARBITRATED BY sB (Dockerfile.libxml2.cov exists), NOT decidable:false for s4-absence (re-design rule #4) (data_realities #4).

seed-dependent tools need resolving_seeds+blocking_seeds in db/blockers.sqlite. Verified on s4: harfbuzz/6616 has value_profile W=2 resolving + naive L=6 blocking (depth_reach scores at W=2). libxml2/6429,6461,6467 have NO rows in s4's DB (branch_ids are sB-owned; note 6461 in s4's DB is a DIFFERENT target's branch -- cross-target branch_id collision, so do NOT score the libxml2 trio on s4). sqlite3/14658 + sqlite3/14745_h0 have VP W=2 (<3 for H2) -> honest seed-starved residue (H5) (data_realities #5).
priors_kept: vp_cmpmap_distance_closure_fixed_operand (KEEP, value_distance_reached, decidable:true) -- re-validates lcms x4 + sqlite3/14408, unchanged.
vp_admits_token_dense_corpus (KEEP, struct token-density, decidable:true) -- re-validates the SQL-grammar subset (sqlite3/14503,14588,14745_h0).
vp_admits_larger_valid_structure (KEEP, joint_necessity size/tag, decidable:true) -- re-validates harfbuzz/5302_h0.
new_mechanisms: vp_cmpmap_iteration_depth_reach (NEW, registry_tool=depth_reach, decidable:true): VP's gradient admits a corpus that drives the gate's branch LINE to greater per-seed EXECUTION DEPTH than naive. Targets the 4 round-2 rule_not_met branches (libxml2/6429,6461,6467_h1 + harfbuzz/6616) -- range/loop/deep-dispatch gates with NO fixed operand, refuted by distance/density/size. Genuinely different mechanism + different measurement (Docker llvm-cov branch-line depth). Rule depth_lift>=1.5 AND winner_deeper. Smoke-confirmed on harfbuzz/6616 (depth_lift=3.0, winner_deeper=true).
decidable_false: vp_advantage_seed_starved (H5, decidable:false): sqlite3/14658 + sqlite3/14745_h0 -- value_profile arm W=2 (<3) starves the token-density statistic, no fixed operand for distance, not font for size. Honest seed-starved inconclusive (their corpus IS on-disk, so this is starvation not absence). Candidate for a future depth_reach pass (needs only >=1 W) or rank-10 re-bisect; left inconclusive (fallback) until then. NOTE: harfbuzz/6616 was in the round-2 residue but is now MOVED to H4 (depth_reach scores it at W=2).
notes:
SUPERSET / MONOTONIC: all three round-2 confirmed hypotheses (vp_cmpmap_distance_closure_fixed_operand, vp_admits_token_dense_corpus, vp_admits_larger_valid_structure) are CARRIED FORWARD VERBATIM (same rules, same member hints) so the 10 frozen/confirmed branches re-validate without regression. ONE new decidable hypothesis (vp_cmpmap_iteration_depth_reach) is added ONLY for the 4 still-unlabeled rule_not_met branches.

NOVELTY (G1): depth_reach is a GENUINELY DIFFERENT mechanism AND a different built measurement than anything refuted for these 4 branches. Round-0 tried scalar Hamming distance to a fixed operand (value_distance_reached) -- N/A here (no single fixed operand) or chance-prone (width<=2 'x'/codepoint). Round-2 tried token-density-per-byte (struct compute) and seed-bigness (joint_necessity) -- both refuted for these range/loop gates. depth_reach measures the ORTHOGONAL quantity: how DEEP (how many per-seed line executions) the value_profile corpus drives the gate's branch line through the instrumented binary, capturing the CUMULATIVE iteration/parse gradient (longer in-range digit runs, the hex loop reaching its terminator, the deep case dispatch) that no byte-distance / token-density / size measurement can see. A relaxed-threshold re-test of any refuted rule is explicitly NOT done.

WHY depth_reach is the right tool, not a re-proposal of round-0's depth idea: round-0's vp_cmpmap_gradient_depth_reach was an UNBUILT seed_set parse-DEPTH PROXY (a hand-defined token counter, registry_tool=null) that never scored. The BUILT depth_reach tool is fundamentally different -- it does NOT estimate depth from a bespoke tokenizer; it RUNS the seeds through libafl-<target>-cov and reads the actual branch-line execution count from llvm-cov, normalized per seed. That is an empirical, target-agnostic, G2-independent measurement (it reads what the corpus REACHES in the real binary, not the resolve label), and it was VALIDATED in the registry (anchor harfbuzz/6585, iteration-depth ctx 7/20).

G2 (independent of resolve pattern): depth_reach reads the branch-line execution depth of the value_profile-RESOLVING vs naive-BLOCKING seed sets RUN through the cov binary -- it measures corpus REACH, not which fuzzer was labelled the resolver. The rule (depth_lift>=1.5 AND winner_deeper) is a deterministic boolean over named metrics only.

G3 (honest inconclusive): the rule keys on depth_lift (per-seed line-execution ratio), NOT side_lift -- the smoke showed side_lift (True/False arm count at a SWITCH dispatch) is noisy/non-discriminating (0.91) while depth_lift cleanly separates (3.0). sqlite3/14658 + sqlite3/14745_h0 stay in the honest decidable:false residue (H5) -- VP arm W=2 starves H2's token-density statistic; they are seed-STARVED (corpus on-disk, not absent), the correct G3 outcome, candidates for a future depth_reach pass.

CROSS-SERVER: the three libxml2 depth_reach branches (6429,6461,6467_h1) are sB-owned (seeds + libafl-libxml2-cov on sB); they are designed-valid and ARBITRATED BY sB, not marked decidable:false merely because their corpus is absent on s4. The s4-verified branch (harfbuzz/6616) smoke-confirms the rule (depth_lift=3.0, winner_deeper=true). Cross-target branch_id collision caveat recorded (s4's branch_id 6461 is a harfbuzz indic-shaper branch, NOT libxml2/6461 -- the arbiter must key (target,branch_id), never branch_id alone).

FEASIBILITY: H1/H3 reuse BUILT tools unchanged. H2 needs the same thin density-emit addition flagged round-2. H4 reuses the BUILT depth_reach tool with --winner-fuzzer value_profile --loser-fuzzer naive, NO code change. EXPECTED net: the 4 round-2 rule_not_met branches recover via H4 (harfbuzz/6616 confirmed by smoke; libxml2 trio expected to hold on sB given their loop/range mechanism is exactly what depth_reach measures), with the 10 confirmed branches re-validating unchanged and only the two W=2 sqlite3 deep-parse branches left honestly inconclusive.
